tossups.question: After his daughter asks to buy a pair of spurs, a character with this profession reminds her of the time she set her classmate Thomas Earl Parkman, Jr. on fire. In a novel, a character with this profession who secretly withdraws money from banks in Copenhagen is named Mundt. To avenge his friend, one character with this job steals a gun from a cop while playing a game of checkers with whiskey bottles as pieces. Characters with this profession are members of (*) “the Circus” in a series of novels. Hawthorne offers this job to a former vacuum cleaner salesman in a novel set in Cuba written by Graham Greene. A man forced to retire from this profession, George Smiley, appears in several novels by John le Carré. For 10 points, name this profession of Ian Fleming’s character James Bond.
tossups.question_sanitized: After his daughter asks to buy a pair of spurs, a character with this profession reminds her of the time she set her classmate Thomas Earl Parkman, Jr. on fire. In a novel, a character with this profession who secretly withdraws money from banks in Copenhagen is named Mundt. To avenge his friend, one character with this job steals a gun from a cop while playing a game of checkers with whiskey bottles as pieces. Characters with this profession are members of (*) "the Circus" in a series of novels. Hawthorne offers this job to a former vacuum cleaner salesman in a novel set in Cuba written by Graham Greene. A man forced to retire from this profession, George Smiley, appears in several novels by John le Carre. For 10 points, name this profession of Ian Fleming's character James Bond.
tossups.answer: spy [accept secret agent; accept intelligence operative; accept The Spy Who Came in from the Cold; prompt on vacuum cleaner salesman before mentioned by asking, “What other job does he have?”] (The Graham Greene novel is Our Man in Havana.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: spy [accept secret agent; accept intelligence operative; accept The Spy Who Came in from the Cold; prompt on vacuum cleaner salesman before mentioned by asking, "What other job does he have?"] (The Graham Greene novel is Our Man in Havana.)

tossups.question: The protagonist of this novel tells his child that people should never “learn from experience,” using the example of a clown who repeatedly falls into a bucket of whitewash. When she was thirteen, a girl in this novel poured lighter fluid on her classmate Thomas Earl Parkman, Jr. and set him on fire after he pulled her hair. While thinking that a man from Nottwich who had trouble undoing a stripper’s corset is becoming too pitiable to kill, the protagonist of this novel pulls out a gun that he stole from a policeman and shoots at him. At a (*) banquet in this novel, a dachshund named Max drinks poisoned alcohol off the floor and dies. This novel’s protagonist takes a microphotograph of a list of names after playing checkers with tiny bourbon bottles to get Captain Segura drunk. Beatrice Severn assists the vacuum cleaner salesman turned spy James Wormold in, for 10 points, what Graham Greene novel set in Cuba?
tossups.question_sanitized: The protagonist of this novel tells his child that people should never "learn from experience," using the example of a clown who repeatedly falls into a bucket of whitewash. When she was thirteen, a girl in this novel poured lighter fluid on her classmate Thomas Earl Parkman, Jr. and set him on fire after he pulled her hair. While thinking that a man from Nottwich who had trouble undoing a stripper's corset is becoming too pitiable to kill, the protagonist of this novel pulls out a gun that he stole from a policeman and shoots at him. At a (*) banquet in this novel, a dachshund named Max drinks poisoned alcohol off the floor and dies. This novel's protagonist takes a microphotograph of a list of names after playing checkers with tiny bourbon bottles to get Captain Segura drunk. Beatrice Severn assists the vacuum cleaner salesman turned spy James Wormold in, for 10 points, what Graham Greene novel set in Cuba?

tossups.question: A novel set in this city has a page filled with the repeated word “blen” and begins with an MC saying,“Showtime!” An ex-radio journalist explores this city’s seedy underground in a trilogy by Pedro Juan Gutiérrez whose title refers to it as “dirty.” Characters such as Arsenio Cue and Bustrófedon frequent this city’s nightclubs. In a novel set in this city, the protagonist’s daughter Milly is courted by Captain Segura, against whom the protagonist plays checkers using miniature whiskey bottles as pieces. This city is the setting of Three Trapped Tigers by Guillermo Cabrera Infante. In a novel set in this city, the design of the “Atomic Pile” is among the schematics passed on to a spy by vacuum cleaner salesman James Wormold. For 10 points, a Graham Greene novel is titled Our Man in what city?
tossups.answer: Havana [or La Habana; accept Our Man in Havana; accept Dirty Havana Trilogy or Trilogía sucia de La Habana]

packet.name: B_ Cambridge A, CWRU A, UCSD A, NCF A

tossups.answer_sanitized: Havana [or La Habana; accept Our Man in Havana; accept Dirty Havana Trilogy or Trilogia sucia de La Habana]

In a novel set in this city, the design of the "Atomic Pile" is among the schematics passed on to a spy by vacuum cleaner salesman James Wormold. For 10 points, a Graham Greene novel is titled Our Man in what city?

tossups.question: A character created by this author notes that “a picture-postcard is a symptom of loneliness” before attempting to call the British Consul while being beaten by two policemen. Before reading the poem “Lord Ullin’s Daughter,” a character in a novel by this author devours raw meat after fighting with an injured dog over a bone. A character uses Lamb’s Tales from Shakespeare to decode messages sent by his boss (*) Hawthorne in a novel by this author that features the secretary Beatrice Severn. While hiding in a barn, the title character of a novel by this author learns Morse code from Coral Fellows, and is later betrayed by a yellow-toothed mestizo while on the run from the lieutenant. For 10 points, what Catholic author created the whiskey priest in his novel The Power and the Glory?
tossups.answer: (Henry) Graham Greene (The other novel is Our Man in Havana.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: (Henry) Graham Greene (The other novel is Our Man in Havana.)

A character uses Lamb's Tales from Shakespeare to decode messages sent by his boss (*) Hawthorne in a novel by this author that features the secretary Beatrice Severn. While hiding in a barn, the title character of a novel by this author learns Morse code from Coral Fellows, and is later betrayed by a yellow-toothed mestizo while on the run from the lieutenant. For 10 points, what Catholic author created the whiskey priest in his novel The Power and the Glory?

tossups.question: The protagonist of this novel plays a game of checkers with whiskey bottle pieces, and he met his secretary when she spilled Coke on a policeman at the Tropicana Club. A dog dies from drinking the protagonist’s poisoned whiskey when he speaks at a luncheon of the (+) European Traders Association. The protagonist communicates with his boss Hawthorne using Lamb’s Tales From Shakespeare as a book code. Police Captain Segura courts the protagonist’s daughter (*) Milly but does not investigate the murder of his friend Dr. Hasselbacher. Drawings of the Atomic Pile are passed off as constructions in Oriente, causing the protagonist and his partner Beatrice Severn to be fired by their bosses in London. For ten points, name this novel about Joe Wormold, a vacuum-cleaner-salesman-turned-spy written by Graham Greene.

Drawings of the Atomic Pile are passed off as constructions in Oriente, causing the protagonist and his partner Beatrice Severn to be fired by their bosses in London. For ten points, name this novel about Joe Wormold, a vacuum-cleaner-salesman-turned-spy written by Graham Greene.

tossups.question: At one point in this novel, the daughter of the protagonist gets a slap on the wrist from her duenna after she sets one of her classmates on fire. After the engineer Cifuentes is shot at, this novel’s protagonist saves the “nude dancer” Teresa from being arrested at the seedy Shanghai Theater. The protagonist of this novel walks in on another character wearing his Uhlan uniform shortly before discovering that character has cracked a code written in a copy of Charles (*) Lamb’s Tales from Shakespeare.This novel’s protagonist accidentally kills a dog with a poisoned glass of whiskey while speaking at a luncheon for the European Traders association, and wins a checkers drinking game against a man who owns a cigarette case made of human skin, Captain Segura. Hawthorne sends Beatrice Severn to aid the title character of this novel after receiving the schematics for the Atomic Pile. For 10 points, name this novel about James Wormold, a vacuum-salesman-turned-spy, written by Graham Greene.

Hawthorne sends Beatrice Severn to aid the title character of this novel after receiving the schematics for the Atomic Pile. For 10 points, name this novel about James Wormold, a vacuum-salesman-turned-spy, written by Graham Greene.

tossups.question: The protagonist of this novel sneaks into a friend's bedroom and finds him wearing tight pants, a helmet, and a breastplate. A character in this novel claims that there is a "torturable class" of people, owns a cigarette case made of human skin, and is sprayed with water while crashing a birthday dinner. In this novel, two characters drive to the Shanghai club and pick up a random stripper after a pilot named Raul is killed in a car crash. In a climactic scene of this novel, a daschund dies after drinking poisoned whiskey at a dinner of the European Trader's Association. To support his Catholic daughter Milly's interest in horse riding, the protagonist takes a job in which he passes off vacuum cleaner schismatics to his boss, Hawthorne. For 10 points, name this novel about James Wormold's misadventures as a spy in Cuba by Graham Greene.
tossups.question_sanitized: The protagonist of this novel sneaks into a friend's bedroom and finds him wearing tight pants, a helmet, and a breastplate. For 10 points, name this novel about James Wormold's misadventures as a spy in Cuba by Graham Greene.

tossups.question: One character in this novel has a cigarette case made of skin from the policeman who tortured his father to death, wishes to marry Milly, and opines that Catholics are more torturable, just as they are more criminal. That man is bested in a checkers game where the pieces are tiny bottles of scotch, allowing the protagonist to steal his gun and kill Carter in revenge for the death of a man who successfully decoded messages using Lamb’s Shakespeare, Dr. (*) Hasselbacher. The protagonist had earlier avoided death at a trade meeting presided over by Dr. Braun, where a lack of carrots on his plate indicates poison. The protagonist earns money from the spy Hawthorne by sketching vacuum cleaner parts and claiming they are military installations in the mountains. For 10 points, name this novel featuring James Wormold, set in Batista’s Cuba and written by Graham Greene.

The protagonist had earlier avoided death at a trade meeting presided over by Dr. Braun, where a lack of carrots on his plate indicates poison. bonuses.leadin: In this novel, two characters play a drinking game of checkers using bottles of Scotch as game pieces. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel that follows James Wormold, a vacuum salesman who fabricates reports of military dealings to the British MI6 service.
This author of Our Man in Havana created a whisky priest who flees from a lieutenant in the Mexican state of Tabasco in his novel The Power and the Glory.

The spy novel The Heat of Day, written by this other author, discusses the feeling of life after the Blitz in its line “vacuum as to future was offset by vacuum as to past.” This author also wrote The Death of the Heart.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this novel, two characters play a drinking game of checkers using bottles of Scotch as game pieces. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel that follows James Wormold, a vacuum salesman who fabricates reports of military dealings to the British MI6 service.
This author of Our Man in Havana created a whisky priest who flees from a lieutenant in the Mexican state of Tabasco in his novel The Power and the Glory.

The spy novel The Heat of Day, written by this other author, discusses the feeling of life after the Blitz in its line "vacuum as to future was offset by vacuum as to past." This author also wrote The Death of the Heart.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about vacuum cleaner salesmen in 20th-century British literature, for 10 points each.
parts: The vacuum cleaner salesman James Wormold fabricates increasingly elaborate intelligence reports for MI6 while in the title city in this comic novel by Graham Greene.

Julian MacLaren-Ross, whose novel Of Love and Hunger is about a vacuum cleaner salesman, was parodied as the writer X. Trapnel in this series. This twelve-volume series opens with the novel A Question of Upbringing.
Don van Vliet (“vleet”), better known as the musician Captain Beefheart, once sold a vacuum cleaner to this author of Brave New World with the convincing sales pitch “I assure you sir, [it] sucks.”
answers: A Dance to the Music of Time (by Anthony Powell)

packet.name: N - Florida A, JHU A, Minnesota B, Queens A, Yale A

answers_sanitized: A Dance to the Music of Time (by Anthony Powell)

Don van Vliet ("vleet"), better known as the musician Captain Beefheart, once sold a vacuum cleaner to this author of Brave New World with the convincing sales pitch "I assure you sir, [it] sucks."

bonuses.leadin: This girl replies that she does not believe in God when a fugitive asks her to pray for him. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this young daughter of a banana plantation owner. She refuses to let a police lieutenant search their property for the whiskey priest.
This author who included Coral Fellows in The Power and the Glory also described the naïve CIA agent Alden Pyle in The Quiet American.
In this other novel by Graham Greene, a salesman named James Wormold is ill-advisedly hired as a secret agent and literally makes his work up as he goes along.
